name: Kente Gold
context: Royal gold of Ashanti kente cloth. Wealth, royalty, high status. Reserved for kings and queen mothers.

name: Yoruba Indigo
context: Deep indigo from adire resist-dye. Symbolises wealth, coolness, spiritual protection. Extracted from elu leaf.

name: Ndebele Red
context: Bold red from Ndebele house murals. Identity, resistance, womanhood. Painted by women to mark life stages.

name: Bogolan Earth
context: Fermented mud on cotton. Bogolan/mudcloth earth tones encode hunting, fertility, and protection symbols.

name: Shea Ochre
context: Golden ochre from shea butter and earth pigments. Used in body painting, pottery, and textile decoration across the Sahel.

name: Nsibidi Black
context: Charcoal black of Nsibidi ideographic writing. Carved into skin and walls by Ekpe secret society. Oldest African script still in use.

name: Tingatinga Vermillion
context: Bright enamel of Tingatinga paintings. Saturated flat colour depicting animals and spirits on masonite board.

name: Ankara Magenta
context: Vivid print of wax-resist ankara fabric. Dutch-origin textile reclaimed as pan-African identity marker.

name: Township Zinc
context: Corrugated zinc grey of township architecture. Beauty from constraint. William Kentridge, Zwelethu Mthethwa.

name: Wangechi Mutu Pink
context: Fleshy pink of Wangechi Mutu's collaged bodies. Nairobi to Brooklyn. Hybridity, gender, post-colonial flesh.

name: Kerry James Marshall Black
context: Ultra-black figures in domestic settings. Marshall's refusal of representational lightening. Blackness as formal strategy.

name: Kehinde Wiley Green
context: Verdant botanical backgrounds of Kehinde Wiley's portraits. Black figures in Old Master frames. Nature as coronation.

name: Lake Retba Pink
context: Lac Rose, Senegal's pink salt lake. Dunaliella salga algae. Natural wonder harvested by Wolof communities.

name: Ibrahim El-Salahi Ochre
context: Amber-ochre of El-Salahi's calligraphic abstraction. Arabic script dissolved into Sudanese landscape. Prison diaries.

name: Julie Mehretu Storm
context: Layered grey of Julie Mehretu's architectural abstractions. Maps, wind patterns, migration. Scale as meaning.

name: Njideka Crosby Collage
context: Warm transfer-print brown of Njideka Akunyili Crosby. Domestic scenes layered with Nigerian magazine transfers. Dual belonging.

name: Tyrian Purple
context: Extracted from murex sea snails. Worth more than gold. Royalty from Carthage to Rome to Byzantium.

name: Alizarin Crimson
context: From madder root (Rubia tinctorum). Ancient Persia through Renaissance. Deep, transparent red of Old Masters.

name: Vermillion
context: Cinnabar mercury sulfide. Sacred red in Chinese lacquer, Roman murals, and Byzantine manuscripts. Zhu sha.

name: Egyptian Blue
context: First synthetic pigment. Calcium copper silicate. Blue of pharaohs, Amun-Ra, and afterlife imagery. 2600 BCE.

name: Terra Cotta
context: Fired earth clay. From Nok terracottas to Greek amphorae to Mexican pottery. Universal material of civilisation.

name: Raw Umber
context: Natural earth pigment from Umbria. Warm brown of Caravaggio shadow, chiaroscuro, and Baroque drama.

name: Burnt Sienna
context: Heat-treated Sienese earth. Warm reddish-brown of Renaissance flesh tones and architectural washes.

name: Naples Yellow
context: Lead antimonate from volcanic soil. Warm yellow of Baroque painting. Tiepolo, Vermeer highlights.

name: Caput Mortuum
context: Dead head. Iron oxide residue from alchemy. Dark violet-brown of monastic manuscripts and memento mori.

name: Picasso Blue Period
context: Melancholic blue of Picasso's 1901-1904 paintings. Poverty, isolation, blindness. The Old Guitarist. Depression as palette.

name: Van Gogh Sunflower
context: Chrome yellow of Vincent's Arles sunflowers. Obsessive joy. Lead chromate pigment darkening over 130 years.

name: Van Gogh Starry Night
context: Swirling cobalt night of Saint-Remy asylum. Turbulence patterns matching fluid dynamics. Madness as mathematics.

name: Monet Water Lily
context: Silvery blue-green of Giverny pond. Monet's 250 water lily paintings. Cataracts shaping late vision into abstraction.

name: Turner Gold
context: Molten gold light of JMW Turner's seascapes. The Fighting Temeraire. Sun dissolving form. Proto-abstraction.

name: Klimt Gold
context: Real gold leaf in The Kiss. Gustav Klimt's Byzantine-meets-Art Nouveau. Ravenna mosaics reborn in Vienna.

name: Caravaggio Shadow
context: Tenebrism. Light from darkness. Caravaggio's cellar-lit scenes. Murder, devotion, and chiaroscuro as theology.

name: Titian Red
context: Rich Venetian red of Titian's flesh and drapery. Glazed in layers. The Assumption, Bacchus. Colour over drawing.

name: Vermeer Pearl
context: Luminous cream-white of Vermeer's light. Girl with a Pearl Earring. Camera obscura glow. Quietude as subject.

name: Whistler Nocturne
context: Blue-grey of Whistler's Thames nocturnes. Art for art's sake. Sued Ruskin for flinging paint. Music as painting.

name: El Greco Storm
context: Livid grey-green of El Greco's Toledo skies. Elongated saints, inner vision. Crete to Spain. Expressionism 300 years early.

name: Hockney Pool
context: California swimming pool blue. David Hockney's A Bigger Splash. Light, desire, surface. Bradford to Los Angeles.

name: Warhol Pop Pink
context: Hot pink of Warhol's Marilyn silkscreens. Mass production as aesthetic. The Factory. Celebrity as commodity.

name: Louise Bourgeois Red
context: Visceral red of Bourgeois's fabric cells and installations. Maman. Memory, sexuality, the body. Red rooms.

name: Agnes Martin Pale
context: Near-white of Agnes Martin's grids. Happiness, innocence, beauty. Pencil lines on gesso. Desert light.

name: Gerhard Richter Grey
context: Squeegee grey of Richter's abstractions. Neither representation nor abstraction. Photography dissolved into paint.

name: Anselm Kiefer Lead
context: Oxidised lead of Kiefer's book sculptures. Alchemy, Holocaust, German mythology. Straw and ash on canvas.

name: Valentino Red
context: Valentino Garavani's signature rosso. Seen at Barcelona opera, 1959. La dolce vita couture. Red as identity.

name: Hermes Orange
context: Originally cream, switched to orange during WWII paper shortage. Now the most recognisable luxury colour in fashion.

name: Tiffany Blue
context: Pantone 1837, trademarked. Charles Lewis Tiffany chose it for the 1837 Blue Book. Robin's-egg. Aspirational turquoise.

name: Schiaparelli Shocking Pink
context: Elsa Schiaparelli's 1937 fragrance colour. Surrealism meets couture. Dalí collaboration. Shocking as manifesto.

name: Chanel Noir
context: The little black dress. Coco Chanel liberated black from mourning. Elegance is elimination. 1926.

name: Sumi Ink
context: Pine soot and animal glue. Zen calligraphy, sumi-e ink wash painting. The infinite gradations of black.

name: Celadon
context: Jade-green glaze of Song dynasty ceramics. Korean celadon ware. Zen simplicity, the colour of heaven.

name: Cinnabar Red
context: Zhu sha. Mercury sulfide pigment. Red lacquer of Chinese temples, seals, and new year. Fortune and joy.

name: Imperial Yellow
context: Exclusive to the emperor. Ming and Qing dynasty roof tiles, dragon robes. The centre of the cosmos.

name: Lapis Lazuli
context: Ultramarine from Afghan mines. More precious than gold. The Virgin Mary's mantle. Vermeer's blue.

name: Ukiyo-e Prussian
context: Berlin blue adopted by Hokusai. The Great Wave. Imported pigment that transformed Edo-period woodblock printing.

name: Raku Smoke
context: Carbon smoke trapped in crackle glaze. Wabi-sabi tea ceremony ware. Chojiro, Sen no Rikyu. Imperfection as beauty.

name: Matcha
context: Powdered green tea colour. Chanoyu tea ceremony. Zen Buddhism, seasonal awareness, ichigo ichie (one chance, one meeting).

name: Kintsugi Gold
context: Gold lacquer repair of broken ceramics. Damage as history, not shame. Wabi-sabi philosophy made material.

name: Batik Wax Brown
context: Hot wax resist-dye on cotton. Javanese court batik. UNESCO intangible heritage. Each pattern has spiritual meaning.

name: Thai Silk Magenta
context: Jim Thompson Thai silk revival. Vibrant mudmee tie-dye. Isan region. Natural dyes from lac insects and bark.

name: Ai Weiwei Grey
context: Concrete and rebar grey of Ai Weiwei's Sichuan earthquake memorials. Surveillance state. Grey as resistance.

name: Saffron
context: Crocus stigma. Sacred yellow of Hindu renunciation, Buddhist robes, and Persian cuisine. 75,000 flowers per pound.

name: Sindoor Red
context: Vermillion powder applied to the parting of married Hindu women's hair. Shakti, Parvati, auspicious power.

name: Neel Indigo
context: Neel/Nila from indigofera plant. Indian subcontinent's gift to world textiles. British exploitation sparked indigo revolts.

name: Turmeric Gold
context: Haldi. Sacred yellow of weddings, temples, and Ayurveda. Purification and fertility across South and Southeast Asia.

name: Madhubani Earth
context: Natural earth pigment of Madhubani painting. Women's art tradition depicting mythology and nature on mud walls.

name: Henna Copper
context: Lawsonia inermis plant paste. Wedding hands, celebration, blessing. 5000 years across MENA and South Asia.

name: Cochineal Crimson
context: Carmine from cochineal insects on nopal cactus. Aztec tribute commodity. Conquered by Spain, it coloured the world.

name: Mayan Blue
context: Indigo fused with palygorskite clay. Survives 1000 years. Used in sacrifice and temple painting. Indestructible pigment.

name: Quetzal Green
context: Colour of the resplendent quetzal. Sacred to Aztec and Maya. Jade, feathers, and divine kingship.

name: Pachamama Terracotta
context: Earth mother colour of Andean ceramics and textiles. Moche, Nazca, Inca pottery traditions.

name: Mendieta Earth
context: Ana Mendieta's Silueta series. Body pressed into earth, fire, blood. Cuban exile, goddess imagery, land art.

name: Wifredo Lam Earth
context: Mud-brown of Wifredo Lam's The Jungle. Afro-Cuban spirit world. Picasso's student who decolonised Cubism.

name: Frida Cobalt
context: Cobalt blue of Casa Azul, Frida Kahlo's home. Mexican blue walls. Pain transformed into visual intensity.

name: Rivera Fresco Sienna
context: Earth pigments of Diego Rivera's murals. Workers, corn, revolution. Wet plaster and pre-Columbian revival.

name: Oiticica Tropical
context: Vivid orange of Helio Oiticica's Parangoles. Samba, favela, participatory art. Tropicalia as liberation.

name: Mola Scarlet
context: Reverse-applique textile art of Guna (Kuna) women. Vivid layers cut to reveal symbolic animal and geometric forms.

name: Torres-Garcia Compass
context: Golden geometry of Joaquin Torres-Garcia. Inverted maps. South is up. Universal constructivism from Montevideo.

name: Persian Turquoise
context: Isfahan mosque tiles. Neyshabur turquoise mines. Protection against the evil eye. The dome of heaven.

name: Iznik Cobalt
context: Cobalt blue of Iznik ceramics. Ottoman mosque tiles, Topkapi Palace. Suleiman's architectural programme.

name: Klein Blue
context: International Klein Blue (IKB). Yves Klein's patented ultramarine. The immaterial made visible. 1960.

name: Rothko Black
context: Mark Rothko's late chapel paintings. Approaching void. The Houston chapel. Contemplation and darkness.

name: Basquiat Crown Gold
context: Jean-Michel Basquiat's crown motif. Self-coronation. Street art to gallery. Black identity as royalty.

name: Vantablack
context: 99.96% light absorption. Anish Kapoor controversy. The blackest black. Art ownership of a colour.

name: Pantone Living Coral
context: 2019 Colour of the Year. Warm, nurturing coral reef. Environmental awareness meets design optimism.

name: Afrofuturist Chrome
context: Metallic silver of Afrofuturist costume and design. Sun Ra, Janelle Monae, Black Panther. Technology as liberation.

name: El Anatsui Copper
context: Recycled bottle caps and copper wire. El Anatsui's shimmering tapestries. Consumption, colonialism, beauty from waste.

name: Prussian Blue
context: First modern synthetic colour. Accidental discovery 1706. Hokusai, Gainsborough, cyanotype photography.

name: Soviet Constructivist Red
context: Revolutionary red of Rodchenko, El Lissitzky, Popova. Beat the Whites with the Red Wedge. Propaganda as art.

name: Bauhaus Primary Red
context: Pure red of Kandinsky's colour theory. Form follows function. Triangle=yellow, circle=blue, square=red.

name: Memphis Pink
context: Ettore Sottsass and Memphis Group. Anti-Bauhaus. Playful, excessive, post-modern. Pink laminate and terrazzo.

name: Ochre
context: Oldest pigment. 300,000 years. Blombos Cave, Lascaux, Aboriginal body paint. Iron oxide earth. Universal human mark-making.

name: Aboriginal Red Ochre
context: Sacred red earth of Dreamtime painting. 65,000 years of continuous tradition. Body ceremonial paint. Uluru.

name: Tapa Bark Cloth
context: Beaten bark cloth (tapa/siapo). Samoan, Tongan, Fijian. Geometric patterns stamped in earth tones. Worn as garment and ceremony.

name: Maori Pounamu
context: New Zealand greenstone (nephrite jade). Sacred to Maori. Taonga (treasure). Hei-tiki amulets and mere weapons.

name: Veve Charcoal
context: Charcoal and ash lines of vodou veve ritual drawings. Ground symbols invoking lwa spirits. Sacred geometry.

name: Carnival Gold
context: Gold sequins and lame of Trinidad Carnival and Rio samba. Masquerade, liberation, embodied resistance.

name: Wong Kar-wai Neon
context: Neon-saturated nights of Chungking Express, In the Mood for Love. Rain-soaked Hong Kong. Longing in colour.

name: Kubrick Red
context: The Shining's blood elevator, 2001's HAL eye, Clockwork Orange's droog red. Menace and control.

name: Film Noir Silver
context: Silver-grey of 1940s noir. Venetian blinds, cigarette smoke, moral ambiguity. Double Indemnity, The Third Man.

name: Wes Anderson Pastel
context: Symmetrical pastels of Grand Budapest Hotel. Nostalgia machines. Pink, mint, and powder blue in perfect composition.

name: Carmine Lake
context: Cochineal or kermes insect pigment on alum base. Glazing medium of Titian, Rubens, Rembrandt. Transparent blood red.

name: Viridian
context: Hydrated chromium oxide. The Impressionist green. Monet's gardens, Cezanne's landscapes. Permanent and transparent.

name: Mars Black
context: Iron oxide black. Industrial replacement for bone black. Soulages' Outrenoir — beyond black. Paint as material.

name: Lamp Black
context: Soot from oil lamps. Oldest manufactured pigment. Rembrandt's shadows, Chinese ink, cave painting. Pure carbon.

name: Saharan Sand
context: Erg dune gold. Tuareg territory. Sand as architecture in Djenne, Timbuktu. Material of the world's largest desert.

name: Dead Sea Mineral
context: Mineral grey of the lowest point on earth. Salt crystalline. Masada fortress. Ancient trade and modern healing.
